info.name: DimensionsPot | BodySize Engine v1.4.0
info.description:
Official Postman collection for the DimensionsPot BodySize Engine API (v1.4.0).

Predicts 130 ISO 7250-1 standardized body dimensions from minimal anthropometric input. No photos, no biometric image processing, fully stateless — GDPR/HIPAA-ready by architecture.

## Quick Setup

1. Open **Edit Collection → Variables** tab
2. Set `rapidapi_key` to your RapidAPI subscription key
3. Subscribe at https://rapidapi.com/d-pot-apps/api/dimensionspot-bodysize-engine
4. Run **Health Check** first to confirm connectivity

---

## Prediction Scenarios

| # | Scenario | Anchor Tier | Bundle |
|---|----------|-------------|--------|
| 01 | Strong Anchors — height + weight | PRIMARY_BOTH (~85/78) | FULL_BODY |
| 02 | Rich Anchors — + waist circumference | PRIMARY_RICH (~87/80) | TORSO |
| 03 | Single Anchor — foot length only | SECONDARY (~74/67) | FULL_BODY |
| 04 | Targeted Dimensions — 4 cherry-picked | PRIMARY_BOTH | custom |
| 05 | Bundle Filter — HAND_ARM only | SECONDARY | HAND_ARM |
| 06 | Regional Calibration — ASIA_PACIFIC | PRIMARY_BOTH | FULL_BODY |
| 07 | Body Build Type — OVERWEIGHT morphing | PRIMARY_BOTH | FULL_BODY |
| 08 | Pediatric Engine — age 7 female | LMS (99/80) | HEAD_FACE |
| 09 | Imperial Input — inches + lbs | PRIMARY_BOTH | TORSO |

---

## Key Rules

- `body_height` is in **millimeters** — 178 cm → `1780` (NOT 178)
- `body_mass` is in **kilograms**
- Imperial input: set `input_unit_system: "imperial"` — lengths in inches, mass in lbs
- Pediatric engine activates automatically when `exact_age ≤ 20`
- Adult engine requires at least one anchor; pediatric requires none
- Allowed regions: `GLOBAL | EUROPE | ASIA_PACIFIC | AFRICA | LATAM | INDIA | MIDDLE_EAST`
- Allowed bundles: `FULL_BODY | TORSO | HAND_ARM | HEAD_FACE | LEGS_FEET`
- Allowed body_build_type: `CIVILIAN | ATHLETIC | OVERWEIGHT`

---

## Documentation

- GitHub: https://github.com/D-P0t/dimensionspot-api
- Data Dictionary (all 130 dimensions): https://github.com/D-P0t/dimensionspot-api/blob/main/DATA_DICTIONARY.md
- RapidAPI: https://rapidapi.com/d-pot-apps/api/dimensionspot-bodysize-engine

variable.description: Your RapidAPI subscription key for DimensionsPot BodySize Engine. Get it at: https://rapidapi.com/d-pot-apps/api/dimensionspot-bodysize-engine
API base URL via RapidAPI gateway — do not change

item.name: Prediction
item.description:
POST /v1/predict — Main prediction endpoint.

9 scenarios covering the full range of API capabilities: strong anchors, rich anchors, single-anchor imputation, cherry-picked dimensions, bundle filtering, regional calibration, body build morphing, pediatric engine, and imperial input.

request.description:
Returns all 130 full-body dimensions at PRIMARY_BOTH confidence tier (BONE ~85, FLESH ~78).

The baseline input pattern: two primary anchors (height + weight), full FULL_BODY output with ISO 7250-1 codes and 95% prediction intervals.

**Anchor tier**: PRIMARY_BOTH
**Engine**: ADULT (Ridge Regression, ANSUR II)
**Expected dimensions**: 130
**confidence_score**: BONE ~85, FLESH ~78

item.name: 02 — Rich Anchors | + waist circumference | TORSO | PRIMARY_RICH

request.description:
Returns 29 TORSO dimensions at PRIMARY_RICH tier. Adding one precision circumference anchor (`waist_circumference_omphalion`) elevates confidence scores across all FLESH dimensions.

`confidence_score_threshold: 75` filters the response to high-confidence dimensions only — the supplied waist anchor is returned as `type: MEASURED` with `confidence_score: 100`.

**Anchor tier**: PRIMARY_RICH
**Engine**: ADULT (EUROPE calibration)
**Expected dimensions**: up to 29 (filtered by threshold ≥75)
**confidence_score**: BONE ~87, FLESH ~80

**Precision circumferences that unlock PRIMARY_RICH**:
`chest_circumference`, `waist_circumference_omphalion`, `hip_circumference`, `neck_circumference`, `wrist_circumference`

item.name: 03 — Single Anchor | foot length only | FULL_BODY | imputation

exec: pm.test("Anchors were imputed (anchors_calculated is true)", function () {

request.description:
Returns 130 full-body dimensions reconstructed entirely via the dynamic imputation engine. `foot_length: 275 mm` is the only anchor — the Ridge Imputer derives `body_height` and `body_mass` from it before inference runs.

`header.anchors_calculated` will be `true`, and `header.calculated_anchors` will show the imputed primary values. Confidence drops to SECONDARY tier (~74/67), reflected uniformly across all returned scores.

**Anchor tier**: SECONDARY
**Engine**: ADULT (EUROPE, ATHLETIC build)
**Expected dimensions**: 130
**confidence_score**: BONE ~74, FLESH ~67

**Anchor bounds for foot_length**: 80–420 mm

exec: pm.test("All requested dimensions are present", function () {

request.description:
Returns exactly the 4 dimensions listed in `specific_dimensions`, spanning two bundles (TORSO + LEGS_FEET). `bundle` is set to `null`.

The response contains only the requested keys — no other dimensions are computed or returned. Use this pattern to minimize response payload in production integrations.

**Use case**: fashion e-commerce size recommendation — chest, natural waist, hip, inseam
**Anchor tier**: PRIMARY_BOTH
**Engine**: ADULT
**Expected dimensions**: 4 exactly

**Tip**: `specific_dimensions` can be combined with a `bundle` — the API returns the union.

Returns the 32 HAND_ARM dimensions only. Anchors are within the HAND_ARM domain, maximising confidence scores for that group. Both anchors are returned as `type: MEASURED` with `confidence_score: 100`.

**Use case**: wearables sizing (smartwatches, rings, gloves), VR controller ergonomics
**Anchor tier**: SECONDARY (hand_length + wrist_circumference)
**Engine**: ADULT (MIDDLE_EAST calibration)
**Expected dimensions**: up to 32

**Anchor bounds**:
- `hand_length`: 60–330 mm
- `wrist_circumference`: 80–400 mm (also unlocks PRIMARY_RICH)

Returns 130 dimensions calibrated to East Asian body proportions. Both `input_origin_region` and `target_region` are set to `ASIA_PACIFIC`: the Universal Translator normalises input anchors to the ANSUR II global baseline (Step A), then shifts output proportions to the ASIA_PACIFIC population (Step B).

**Cross-regional example**: Asian customer, European brand → set `input_origin_region: "ASIA_PACIFIC"`, `target_region: "EUROPE"`. The two fields are fully independent.

**Use case**: global fashion platform serving regional size charts
**Anchor tier**: PRIMARY_BOTH
**Engine**: ADULT (ASIA_PACIFIC calibration)
**Expected dimensions**: 130

item.name: 07 — Body Build Type | OVERWEIGHT morphing | FULL_BODY

request.description:
Returns 130 dimensions with circumferences morphed for higher body fat distribution. `body_build_type: OVERWEIGHT` activates BMI-stratified NHANES morphing — the output differs from `CIVILIAN` primarily in circumference dimensions (waist, hip, chest, thighs).

The ANSUR II training baseline is military (lean). NHANES Deurenberg-based morphing adjusts the baseline toward civilian BMI and body fat distribution; OVERWEIGHT further shifts the circumference envelope.

**Use case**: plus-size fashion, bariatric medical equipment sizing
**Anchor tier**: PRIMARY_BOTH
**Engine**: ADULT (OVERWEIGHT morphing)
**Expected dimensions**: 130

**Available body_build_type values**: `CIVILIAN` | `ATHLETIC` | `OVERWEIGHT`

item.name: 08 — Pediatric Engine | age 7 female | HEAD_FACE | LMS + Ridge

exec: pm.test("anchors_calculated is false for pediatric", function () {

request.description:
Returns 20 HEAD_FACE dimensions for a 7-year-old female. No anchors required — the PEDIATRIC engine derives `body_height` and `body_mass` from CDC LMS growth charts for the given age and sex, then runs the full prediction pipeline.

**Engine routing**: `exact_age: 7.0 ≤ 20` → PEDIATRIC engine (LMS Box-Cox, CDC/WHO)

**Confidence score rules in pediatric mode**:
- LMS-derived: `body_height`, `body_mass` → `confidence_score: 99` (exact tabular norm)
- `head_circumference` (ages 0–36 months) → `confidence_score: 99`
- All Ridge-scaled dimensions → capped at `confidence_score: 80`

**`header.anchors_calculated`** is always `false` for pediatric — the LMS method is not the imputation pipeline.
**`header.calculated_anchors`** is always `{}` for pediatric.

**Pediatric age categories**: `INFANT` (0–2y) | `TODDLER` (2–4y) | `CHILD` (4–9y) | `PRE_TEEN` (9–12y) | `TEEN` (12–18y)

**Use case**: childrenswear sizing, children's helmet/eyewear fitting, pediatric ergonomic design

item.name: 09 — Imperial Input | height in inches + mass in lbs | output imperial

exec: pm.test("Output unit is in (imperial output)", function () {

request.description:
Demonstrates full imperial input/output pipeline. Anchors are supplied in inches and lbs; the API converts to metric internally (before validation) and returns results in inches/lbs.

`body_height: 70.1 inches` → 1780.5 mm internally
`body_mass: 180.8 lbs` → ~82.0 kg internally

This request is equivalent to scenario 01 (Strong Anchors) — same subject, same expected dimensions, different unit system. Useful for side-by-side comparison.

**Imperial conversion rules (applied server-side before validation)**:
- All `_height`, `_length`, `_breadth`, `circumference`, `_depth`, `_arc`, `_width`, `_reach`, `span` keys: multiply by 25.4 (inches → mm)
- `body_mass`: divide by 2.20462 (lbs → kg)

**Anchor validation** uses metric bounds. Supplying `body_height: 7` (inches) would be converted to 177.8 mm — valid. Supplying `body_height: 6` (inches) → 152.4 mm — valid (above 400 mm minimum? No — 152 mm is below the 400 mm minimum, so it would return 422).

**Output**: `unit_system: "imperial"` returns all dimensions in inches; `body_mass` in lbs.

item.name: System
item.description:
Health probe, capability discovery, and pre-built request examples.

These endpoints require no request body and are safe to call at any time. `GET /v1/info` response is safe to cache — values do not change at runtime.

request.description:
Liveness/readiness probe. Run this first to confirm connectivity and that the API is fully operational.

Actively verifies all critical runtime dependencies:
- Data directory exists
- Models directory exists
- NASA biological limits file exists

Returns `503` if anything is missing — the orchestrator will pause traffic until the instance is ready.

**Expected response**:
```json
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "1.4.0",
  "models_loaded": true,
  "data_files_ok": true,
  "uptime_seconds": 3721
}
```

Returns static metadata about all API capabilities. Safe to cache — values do not change at runtime.

Returns:
- Supported regions (7): `AFRICA`, `ASIA_PACIFIC`, `EUROPE`, `GLOBAL`, `INDIA`, `LATAM`, `MIDDLE_EAST`
- Supported bundles (5): `FULL_BODY`, `HAND_ARM`, `HEAD_FACE`, `LEGS_FEET`, `TORSO`
- Supported age categories (6): `ADULT`, `CHILD`, `INFANT`, `PRE_TEEN`, `TEEN`, `TODDLER`
- Pediatric age categories (5): `CHILD`, `INFANT`, `PRE_TEEN`, `TEEN`, `TODDLER`
- Supported body build types (3): `ATHLETIC`, `CIVILIAN`, `OVERWEIGHT`
- Supported calculation models (3): `ADULT`, `AUTO`, `PEDIATRIC`
- Supported unit systems: `IMPERIAL`, `METRIC`
- Adult limits standard: NASA-STD-3001
- Pediatric limits: CDC P1–P99 age-banded bounds
- Available endpoints

**Use this endpoint to validate integration configuration before making prediction calls.**

exec: pm.test("Each example has a payload", function () {

request.description:
Returns 8 pre-built request payloads for `POST /v1/predict`. Each payload is immediately usable — copy the `payload` object and POST it to `/v1/predict`.

The 8 scenarios covered:
1. Strong anchors — height + weight, 130 dimensions
2. Rich anchors — height + weight + waist circumference, PRIMARY_RICH tier
3. Single anchor — foot length only, imputation chain
4. Targeted dimensions — 4 named dimensions, cross-bundle
5. Bundle filter — HAND_ARM, 32 dimensions
6. Regional calibration — Asia Pacific, female
7. Body build type — OVERWEIGHT, BMI-adjusted
8. Pediatric model — age 7, female, LMS + Ridge hybrid
